description: Business Economics introduces the core economic principles and how these can be used in a business environment to help inform decision-making and behaviour. Canonical representation of the official IFoA CB2 Syllabus for the 2026 Examinations (April 2025).

metadata.source: IFoA CB2 Syllabus for the 2026 Examinations
metadata.assessment: One online written examination paper; 3h20m including reading time

sections.title: Economic schools, the economic way of thinking and recent historical applications
sections.description: An introduction to economic concepts and models, their use in business as well as recent economic and financial history.

topics.title: Relationship between economics and business
topics.description: Relationship between economics and business

learning_objectives.description: Principles of economic choice, including opportunity cost and scarcity
Core economic concepts used by businesses to make choices relevant to selection of outputs, inputs, technology, location and competition
Features of and differences between microeconomics and macroeconomics

topics.title: Main economic schools and their key features
topics.description: Main economic schools and their key features

learning_objectives.description: Classical school and its key features
Marxian socialism and its key features
Neo-classical, Keynesian, neo-Keynesian and post-Keynesian schools and their key features
Monetarist school and its key features
Austrian school and its key features

topics.title: Recent macroeconomic history
topics.description: Recent macroeconomic history

learning_objectives.description: Progress of the world economy since the Great Depression, with a particular focus on banking crises, their consequences and irrational behaviour

Banking crisis of 2008, the Great Recession, and recovery, including effectiveness of monetary policy in the 2008 financial crisis; governments’ actions to combat recession; aftershocks in Europe; and the stimulus–austerity debate and regulatory action after the 2008 crisis

sections.title: Microeconomics – the behaviour of consumers, firms and markets
sections.description: Understand, in detail, models of consumer choice, the theory of the firm and how these affect business decisions under different conditions.

topics.title: How competitive markets operate
topics.description: How competitive markets operate

learning_objectives.description: Role of the price mechanism and the behaviour of firms and consumers in a free market
Factors that influence market demand and supply
How market equilibrium quantity and price are achieved
How markets react to changes in demand and supply
Calculation of price and income elasticities of demand and price elasticity of supply, using both original and average quantities
Factors that affect elasticity
Effect of elasticity on the short- and long-term operation of markets
How firms deal with risk and uncertainty about future market movements
Price expectations and speculation and how price bubbles develop

topics.title: Consumer demand and behaviour
topics.description: Consumer demand and behaviour

learning_objectives.description: The concept of utility and the representation of consumer preferences as indifference curves
How rational choice and optimal consumption choice is determined by using indifference curves and budget lines
Rational choice, perfect information and irrational behaviour in the context of behavioural economics

topics.title: The impact of advertising on sales and demand
topics.description: The impact of advertising on sales and demand

learning_objectives.description: The impact of advertising on sales and demand

topics.title: Impact of the production function, costs of production and revenue and profit on a firm’s price and output decisions
topics.description: Impact of the production function, costs of production and revenue and profit on a firm’s price and output decisions

learning_objectives.description: The production function and the relationship between short- and long-term inputs and outputs
Average and marginal physical product
Meaning and measurement of costs and how these vary with short- and long-term output
Total, average and marginal costs
Economies of scale and how a business can achieve efficiency in selecting the level of its inputs
Revenue and profit and how they are influenced by market conditions
Calculation of average and marginal revenue
Measurement of profit and how a firm arrives at its profit-maximising output
The ‘shut-down’ point in the short and long run

topics.title: Profit maximisation under perfect competition and monopoly
topics.description: Profit maximisation under perfect competition and monopoly

learning_objectives.description: What determines the market power of a firm
Main features of a market characterised by perfect competition, including how output and price are determined in the short and long term
Monopolies and how they emerge; how they select profit-maximising price and output; and the determination of the profit of a monopolist
Barriers to entry and market contestability and their impact on the profit of a monopolist

topics.title: Profit maximisation under imperfect competition
topics.description: Profit maximisation under imperfect competition

learning_objectives.description: Behaviour of firms under monopolistic competition and why in this type of market only normal profits are made in the long run
Main features of an oligopoly and the behaviour of firms in an oligopoly
Competition and collusion of firms in an oligopoly and how strategic decisions of such firms can be explained by game theory
Behaviour of oligopolists and the consumer interest

topics.title: Pricing strategies that firms in the financial services sector can adopt
topics.description: Pricing strategies that firms in the financial services sector can adopt

learning_objectives.description: How prices are determined in practice and factors that affect the ability of a firm to determine its prices
Average-cost pricing and price discrimination
Pricing strategy for multiple products and how pricing varies with the stage in the life of a product

sections.title: Macroeconomics and the role of government
sections.description: Understand aggregate economic variables and concepts which relate them to government policy, business decision making and financial market variables.

topics.title: Government intervention in a market
topics.description: Government intervention in a market

learning_objectives.description: Extent to which businesses meet the interests of consumers and society in general
Socially efficient perfect markets and why markets fail to achieve the theoretical ideal of social efficiency
Why externalities can lead to inefficient markets
How governments intervene in markets to influence business behaviour and the drawbacks of such intervention
Role of taxation and regulation in correcting shortcomings in markets
Why government intervention might not improve market outcomes in practice even if the existence of ‘market failures’ suggest they can in theory
Policy instruments that can be used to promote environmental sustainability

topics.title: Relationship between the government and the individual firm
topics.description: Relationship between the government and the individual firm

learning_objectives.description: Main targets of ‘competition policy’ and the extent to which it is effective
Why a free market can fail to achieve the optimal amount of research and development
Government intervention to encourage technological advance and innovation

topics.title: Globalisation and multinational business
topics.description: Globalisation and multinational business

learning_objectives.description: Globalisation and its impact on business
The driving processes of globalisation and the benefits of globalisation

topics.title: Importance of international trade
topics.description: Importance of international trade

learning_objectives.description: Growth of international trade and its benefits to countries and firms
Advantages of specialisation
Arguments for trade restrictions and protection of domestic industries
Role of the World Trade Organization (WTO) in international trade

topics.title: Impact of the macroeconomic environment on business
topics.description: Impact of the macroeconomic environment on business

learning_objectives.description: Main macroeconomic variables that governments seek to control
Impact of an economic stimulus on business output
Difference between actual and potential growth
Factors that determine economic growth and the reasons for differences in different nations’ growth rates
Why economies experience periods of boom followed by periods of recession and the factors that influence the length and magnitude of the phases of a business cycle
Causes and costs of unemployment and its impact on the level of business activity
Determination of the price level in the economy by the interaction between aggregate supply (AS) and aggregate demand (AD) in a simple AS–AD model
Causes and costs of inflation and its impact on the level of business activity
Gross domestic product (GDP) and its measurement
Representation of the economy as a simple model of the circular flow of income

topics.title: Balance of payments and the determination of exchange rates
topics.description: Balance of payments and the determination of exchange rates

learning_objectives.description: Definitions of ‘the balance of payments’ and how trade and financial movements affect it
How exchange rates are determined and their impact on business
Relationship between the balance of payments and exchange rates
Advantages and disadvantages of fixed and floating exchange rates
How governments and/or central banks seek to influence the exchange rates and the implications of such actions for other macroeconomic policies and for business
Purpose and effectiveness of monetary union and single currencies, with reference to the European Economic and Monetary Union, the Exchange Rate Mechanism and the creation of a single Currency

topics.title: Role of money and interest rates in the economy
topics.description: Role of money and interest rates in the economy

learning_objectives.description: Functions of money
Factors that determine the amount of money in the economy, what causes it to grow and the role of banks in this process
Concept of the money multiplier
Determination of interest rates
Relationship between money and interest rates
Why central banks play a crucial role in the functioning of economies
How a change in the money supply and/or interest rates affects the level of business activity

topics.title: Role, structure and stability of the financial system
topics.description: Role, structure and stability of the financial system

learning_objectives.description: Functions of the financial sector
Role and functions of investment funds, banks and insurance companies/pension funds
Different ways banks and insurance companies can be exposed to credit risk and liquidity risks through bank loans, corporate bonds, securitisations, syndicated loans and credit derivatives
Why the banking sector is more likely to be exposed to systemic risk than the non-bank financial sector
How financial innovation could lead to some functions of the banking sector being performed by non-banks

topics.title: Factors that determine the level of business activity and how they also affect unemployment and inflation
topics.description: Factors that determine the level of business activity and how they also affect unemployment and inflation

learning_objectives.description: Determination of the equilibrium level of income within a simple aggregate demand–expenditure model
Concept of the multiplier and the calculation of its value
Impact of a rise in money supply on output and prices
Relationship between unemployment and inflation and whether the relationship is stable
How business and consumer expectations affect the relationship between unemployment and inflation and the formation of such expectations
Independent central banks, inflation targeting and their impact on unemployment, business cycles and growth
Course of a business cycle and its turning points
Whether the business cycle is caused by changes in aggregate demand or changes in aggregate supply (or both)

topics.title: Impact of macroeconomic policies on businesses
topics.description: Impact of macroeconomic policies on businesses

learning_objectives.description: Types of macroeconomic policy that are likely to have an impact on business and the way in which this impact takes effect
Impact of fiscal policy on the economy and business and the factors that determine its effectiveness in smoothing out economic fluctuations
Fiscal rules and their efficacy
How monetary policy works in the UK and the eurozone and the roles of the Bank of England and the European Central Bank
Targeting inflation to influence interest rates and economic activity
The merits of central banks following a simple inflation target and possible alternatives

topics.title: Supply-side policies and their impact on businesses
topics.description: Supply-side policies and their impact on businesses

learning_objectives.description: Effect of supply-side policies on business and the economy
Types of supply-side policies that can be pursued and their effectiveness
Impact of cutting taxes on business
Major forms of government policy that can be used to encourage competition
